Source_Species.reason: MdPGT1 is an apple gene used/referenced in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper concerns apple, identified as Malus × domestica.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The study uses 'Royal Gala' for transgenic apple lines, but MdPGT1 itself is referenced from prior work and not explicitly stated here as cloned from 'Royal Gala'.

Applied_Species.reason: MdPGT1 was used in transient assays and referenced in apple transgenic context.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Apple in this paper is Malus × domestica.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Transgenic apple analyses in this paper were performed in 'Royal Gala'.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MdPGT1 is described as converting phloretin into stable glycosylated phlorizin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Phlorizin is discussed as a dihydrochalcone/flavonoid product; DHCs are stated to be flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states DHCs broadly have health benefits and says phlorizin affects glucose absorption/excretion, but does not directly assign all listed functions specifically to MdPGT1-derived phlorizin in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: In transient assays, pHEX2_PGT1 was used to convert reactive phloretin to stable phlorizin for detection.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are stated: MdPGT1 was co-expressed in N. benthamiana to convert phloretin to phlorizin; this enabled DHC detection; MdPGT1 expression was not consistently affected in NCR RNAi lines.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper used transient co-expression in N. benthamiana and HPLC/UPLC metabolite analyses involving MdPGT1 context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Phlorizin/DHCs are reported in leaves, roots, stems, and young fruit.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This is interpretive/meta commentary, not a factual statement directly made by the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative transcriptomics is in the paper, but not specifically for MdPGT1; metabolite profiling is supported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states prior MdPGT1 downregulation caused severe developmental abnormalities.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdPGT1 functions as a glycosyltransferase converting phloretin to phlorizin; UDP-glucosyltransferase designation is consistent with the gene name and function, though domain not deeply characterized here.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: MdPGT1 was used in transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pHEX2 is described as CaMV 35S:ORF:nos-3′ for transient constructs including pHEX2_PGT1.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports engineering DHC biosynthesis generally, but does not explicitly single out MdPGT1 in this exact application statement within Results.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: MdNCR1a is explicitly assigned to MD05G1167400 and accession PP495130.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Apple is given as Malus × domestica.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Although transgenic work used 'Royal Gala', the gene model itself is not explicitly said to originate from Royal Gala.

Applied_Species.reason: MdNCR1a was tested in apple and N. benthamiana assays, with apple as the focal species.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied apple species is Malus × domestica.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Apple transgenic lines were generated in 'Royal Gala'.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies MdNCR1 genes as candidate naringenin chalcone reductases (NCRs), i.e. naringenin chalcone carbon double-bond reductases.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MdNCR1a functions in the phenylpropanoid/flavonoid side branch producing dihydrochalcones.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper centers on DHC biosynthesis as a branch of the flavonoid pathway.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: MdNCR1a is implicated at the branch toward DHC formation via reduction of naringenin chalcone.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper identifies naringenin chalcone as the substrate of NCR activity; MdNCR1a is included among highly active NCR1 genes.

Primary_Product.reason: NCR activity is described as producing phloretin from naringenin chalcone.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This reaction is the central proposed function of MdNCR1 genes, supported by transient assays and MdNCR1b biochemistry.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Phloretin is converted to phlorizin and other DHC glycosides; this direct precursor relationship is explicit.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The Discussion states MdNCR1 fulfills the requirements to be the missing enzyme required for DHC biosynthesis in apple leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MdNCR1a activity was linked to phlorizin accumulation in transient assays and NCR knockdown reduced phlorizin in apple.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Phlorizin belongs to DHCs, and DHCs are described as flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper discusses DHC/phlorizin roles in defense and human health largely in Discussion, but not as a direct Results-supported function of MdNCR1a.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Transient MdNCR1a expression produced very high phlorizin in N. benthamiana.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points match the Results, though RNAi was against NCR genes collectively rather than MdNCR1a alone.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Comparative transcriptomics, transient expression, RNAi knockdown, and metabolite profiling were all used.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: MdNCR1a-GFP localized to the vacuolar membrane in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Transient pHEX2 constructs and RNAi pTKO2 used CaMV 35S promoter.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states MdNCR1a-c share >98% amino acid identity and identical substrate-binding pockets.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR measured MdNCR1a-c together, not MdNCR1a individually; the pattern applies to combined MdNCR1 expression.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same issue: expression was measured collectively for MdNCR1 genes, not specifically MdNCR1a.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Leaf developmental stage and transient expression in N. benthamiana were examined.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative transcriptomics and metabolite profiling methods were used to identify/test MdNCR1a.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdNCR1a is described as a quinone-oxidoreductase homolog in the NCR1 clade.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: DHC/phlorizin accumulation is reported in leaves, roots, stems, and young fruit.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly highlights opportunities to manipulate DHC content in apple and other fruits and applications in biopharming/biofermentation.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: MdNCR1b is explicitly assigned to MD05G1167600 and PP495131.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The gene itself is not explicitly tied to Royal Gala origin, though functional work used Royal Gala plants.

Applied_Species.reason: MdNCR1b was assayed in apple context and in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied apple species is Malus × domestica.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MdNCR1b is part of the MdNCR1 naringenin chalcone reductase group.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It acts in the flavonoid side branch leading to DHCs.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The relevant branch is DHC biosynthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: MdNCR1b diverts naringenin chalcone toward DHC formation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Recombinant MdNCR1b readily reduced naringenin chalcone.

Primary_Product.reason: The product phloretin was identified by LC-MS/MS.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly shows NADPH-dependent reduction of naringenin chalcone to phloretin and weaker p-coumaroyl-CoA reduction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Phloretin is the aglycone precursor to phlorizin and related DHC glycosides.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The study positions MdNCR1 enzymes as the missing key enzyme for leaf DHC biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MdNCR1b transient expression produced high phlorizin; NCR knockdown reduced leaf phlorizin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: These functions are discussed for DHC/phlorizin generally, not demonstrated specifically for MdNCR1b in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: MdNCR1b strongly promoted DHC accumulation and directly formed phloretin in vitro.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by Results.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for MdNCR1b or the NCR group.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: MdNCR1b-GFP localized to the vacuolar membrane.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All kinetic values and substrate scope statements are explicitly reported for recombinant MdNCR1b.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: MdNCR1b-specific expression was not separated from MdNCR1a/c; RT-qPCR was for pooled MdNCR1 expression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The temperature and pH behavior and transient expression conditions are explicitly reported.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative transcriptomics plus LC-MS, GC-MS, HPLC, and UPLC are all in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdNCR1b is a quinone oxidoreductase-like reductase in the NCR1 clade.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Phlorizin/DHCs were measured in leaves, roots, stems, and young fruit tissues.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper proposes engineering DHC production, including in biofermentation and biopharming.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: NCR downregulation redirected flux to flavonol glycosides and reduced total phenolics to 33%–39% of WT.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MdNCR101 is designated an NCR-related gene and showed weak NCR activity.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is discussed in the DHC biosynthesis branch.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: This is within the DHC biosynthetic branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As a weaker paralog, MdNCR101 is reasonably described as a potential auxiliary branch enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: MdNCR101 produced phlorizin in planta, consistent with naringenin chalcone reduction, but its substrate was not directly assayed biochemically.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Weak support from transient in planta phlorizin accumulation, but no direct enzymology for MdNCR101 reducing naringenin chalcone is shown.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Its transient activity links it to phlorizin formation via the phloretin intermediate, albeit inferentially.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish MdNCR101 as the major control point and instead emphasizes NCR1 genes.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Phlorizin is the quantified terminal product in transient assays.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: General DHC function statements are not specifically demonstrated for MdNCR101.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: First two statements are supported; the third ('may contribute to residual DHC production in fruit') is presented as a Discussion possibility, not a demonstrated Result.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient expression, localization, and expression analysis were performed for MdNCR101.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: MdNCR101-GFP localized to the vacuolar membrane.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The relevant constructs were under CaMV 35S promoter systems.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states that 7 of 15 substrate-pocket residues differ from MdNCR1a-c in MdNCR101.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR showed low expression of MdNCR101 in all tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same as above; low expression in all tissues including fruit is directly reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient expression in N. benthamiana is explicitly reported.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative transcriptomics and metabolite profiling were used.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdNCR101 is a quinone oxidoreductase-like reductase homolog.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Phlorizin/DHCs were measured in leaves, roots, stems, and young fruit overall, but attributing low-level fruit accumulation specifically to MdNCR101 is speculative.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This exact 'secondary candidate for fruit engineering' claim is speculative and not directly stated in Results.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly calls MdHCDBR a hydroxycinnamoyl CoA double-bond reductase.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MdHCDBR is discussed as part of a proposed alternative DHC biosynthetic route.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It belongs to the proposed p-coumaroyl-CoA reduction route.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: MdHCDBR acts upstream in the proposed alternative branch by reducing p-coumaroyl-CoA.

Primary_Product.reason: The product dihydro-p-coumaroyl-CoA is explicitly stated.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper shows recombinant MdHCDBR reduced p-coumaroyl-CoA but did not reduce naringenin chalcone to phloretin.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MdHCDBR is presented as supplying a proposed precursor for an alternative DHC route, but not the major leaf pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper concludes MdHCDBR and related ARLs are not responsible for high DHC concentrations in apple leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The pathway context is phlorizin/DHC production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Possible defense/health roles are discussed generally, and the specific tissue-conditional contribution of MdHCDBR remains unresolved.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi downregulation did not reduce foliar phlorizin, supporting this statement.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by Results and Discussion of the present experiments.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RNAi, RT-qPCR, HPLC, and recombinant enzyme/LC-MS assays were used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: RNAi construct used pTKO2 with CaMV 35S promoter; transient expression constructs used pHEX2 with CaMV 35S.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MdCHS, MdPGT1 and MdPGT2 are mentioned only in prior co-expression context from cited work, not as direct protein interactions demonstrated in this paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Possible contribution to fruit DHC production is posed as a future possibility, not demonstrated.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same expression context is given for leaves and fruit.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Leaf tissue, transient N. benthamiana context, and in vitro assays are all described.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR and metabolite profiling were used; no broader omics claim is made here.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is described as an NADPH-dependent double-bond reductase.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaves are clearly relevant; possible fruit contribution is speculative and not resolved by the paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper's data support that MdHCDBR is less suitable than NCR1 for high foliar DHC engineering, while retaining interest for alternative pathway studies.
